subject: Physics
title: The Big Rip
description: A fast breakdown of The Big Rip for builders.

hook.text: If dark energy grows stronger over time, it could eventually tear apart galaxies, stars, atoms, and spacetime itself.

buildup.text: Currently, dark energy is constant. But some models suggest it could intensify—called phantom energy.

discovery.text: With phantom energy, expansion accelerates until gravitational bonds can no longer hold structures together.

twist.text: First galaxy clusters separate. Then galaxies rip apart. Then solar systems. Then planets. Then atoms.

climax.text: In the final fraction of a second, spacetime itself tears apart. Everything ends at once.

punchline.text: Dark energy gone wild. Tearing everything apart, down to the atoms.

quiz.question: What would cause a Big Rip?
options: Dark energy growing stronger over time until it tears apart all structures
A collision between our universe and another
All black holes merging into one
